settings.name: Start date
settings.description: Select the start date for your project. If left empty, the start date will be the first deploy_on_timestamp date.

settings.name: End date
settings.description: Select the end date for your project. If left empty, the end date will be the last deploy_off_timestamp date.

settings.name: Fix empty start times
settings.description: Define how to deal with NA start dates (deploy_on_timestamp).

settings.name: Fix empty end times
settings.description: Define how to deal with NA end dates (deploy_off_timestamp).

options.displayText: Replace NA deploy_off_timestamp with current date

settings.name: Censor capture-related mortality
settings.description: If necessary, input number of days post-capture to exclude from analysis.

settings.name: Perform analysis on subset of data
settings.description: If interested in performing analyses on a subset of the data, select the variable to subset by below. You will define the group of interest within this subset in the next step. Note: if using a survival year or life stage filter, be sure to enter survival year start dates in the following filters. For life stage, your data will neect to contain the column `animal_birth_hatch_year` and you will additionally need to add auxiliary life stage information (template csv files can be found on this app's GitHub page).

settings.name: Define your subsetting condition
settings.description: Enter the factor of the above category you would like the subset your data on (e.g., `m` if `sex` is selected above, to include only male individuals). For 'survival year', enter the desired year (e.g., 2015, 2024). For other parameters, you will need the exact spelling,  capitalization, and/or formatting as your desired subset variable.

settings.name: Perform analysis on a further subset of data
settings.description: If interested in performing analyses on a further subset of the data, select the variable to subset by below. You will define the group of interest within this subset in the next step. Note: if using a survival year or life stage filter, be sure to enter survival year start dates in the following filters. For life stage, your data will neect to contain the column `animal_birth_hatch_year` and you will additionally need to add auxiliary life stage information (template csv files can be found on this app's GitHub page).

settings.name: Define your second subsetting condition
settings.description: Enter the factor of the above category you would like the subset your data on (e.g., `m` if `sex` is selected above, to include only male individuals). For 'survival year', enter the desired year (e.g., 2015, 2024). For other parameters, you will need the exact spelling,  capitalization, and/or formatting as your desired subset variable.

settings.name: Groups for comparison
settings.description: If interested in comparing across groups, identify the grouping variable. Note that `survival year` and `life stage` require entry of additional and/or auxiliary data.

settings.name: Survival year start
settings.description: If comparing across survival years, select the start date of your survival year. Note that what is important is the day and month; year is irrelevant.

settings.name: Animal birth/hatch year definitions
settings.description: For survival year analyses that include animal life stage, upload a spreadsheet containing the following information: animal_life_stage, year_at_start (that is, a file that maps age to lifestage; see template on GitHub). The app expects a file with the extension: `.csv`.

settings.name: Generate periods for life table
settings.description: Define how often to calculate survival probabilities for the life table (e.g., every 30 days).

references.note: Kaplan, E. L.; Meier, P. 1958. Nonparametric estimation from incomplete observations. J. Amer. Statist. Assoc. 53 (282): 457–481
